---
title: "local_linearization"
generated: true
description: "Integrate $dy/dt = f(t, y)$ by exponential / local linearisation."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.numerics.ode`](../nitrix-numerics-ode.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:local_linearization -->
```python
local_linearization(f: VectorField, y0: Float[Array, 'n'], t: Float[Array, 't']) -> Float[Array, 't n']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:local_linearization -->
Integrate $dy/dt = f(t, y)$ by exponential / local linearisation.

The Ozaki local-linearisation scheme (the integrator behind SPM's
`spm_int`): each step linearises $f$ about the current state and
advances the linear part *exactly* with a matrix exponential.  Unlike the
explicit [`euler`](../nitrix-numerics-ode/euler.qmd) / [`midpoint`](../nitrix-numerics-ode/midpoint.qmd) / [`rk4`](../nitrix-numerics-ode/rk4.qmd) steppers it is
**A-stable**, so it integrates **stiff** systems -- such as the
Balloon--Windkessel haemodynamic model -- at a step size where an explicit
method would need a far finer grid or diverge outright.  It is
**exact (to round-off)** for an affine-autonomous field
$f(y) = A y + b$.

Same `f(t, y)` / close-over-args contract as [`euler`](../nitrix-numerics-ode/euler.qmd), with two
differences from the explicit steppers:

- `y0` must be a **flat 1-D** state $(n,)$ -- the step forms the
  $(n, n)$ Jacobian $df/dy$.  For a batched / multi-region
  system, `jax.vmap` this integrator over the batch axis.
- the input is held constant across each step (piecewise-constant /
  frozen-input linearisation), so accuracy on a fast-varying drive is
  controlled, as for the explicit methods, by how densely `t` is sampled.

Differentiable straight through the `scan` (the matrix exponential is
pure matmul); composes with the shipped [`nitrix.linalg.matrix_exp`](../nitrix-linalg.qmd).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:local_linearization -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `f` | `VectorField` | — | — | Vector field `f(t, y) -> dy/dt` (`t` scalar, `y` the flat 1-D state of shape `(n,)`). |
| `y0` | `Float` | `n` | — | Initial state at `t[0]`, a flat 1-D array of shape `(n,)`. |
| `t` | `Float` | `t` | — | Strictly monotone time points of shape `(t,)`; one linearised step is taken per interval. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `t` × `n`. States at each time point, shape `(len(t), n)` (`ys[0]` is `y0`).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:local_linearization -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `t` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
